judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports are very close and hit expectations a, b, and c equally well; Report 1 is stronger on (d) because it explicitly recommends dropping the hardcoded count in favor of the code as source of truth, while Report 2 merely updates the number from 4 to 5 without addressing the drift-prone hardcoding itself.

report_1.evidence: medibill/backend/app/api/invoices.py:4 (import chargekit) and :14 (client = chargekit.Client(api_key_env="CHARGEKIT_API_KEY"))... medibill/backend/app/workers/dunning.py:2 (import chargekit) and :9 (chargekit.Client(api_key_env="CHARGEKIT_API_KEY").charge(...))... medibill/backend/app/billing/errors.py:6 also imports from chargekit... but that's the doc's own named exception — it imports only exception classes for re-export, never calls the SDK, so it's not a violation.

report_2.evidence: medibill/backend/app/api/invoices.py:4 and :14 — imports chargekit directly and constructs its own chargekit.Client(...)... medibill/backend/app/workers/dunning.py:2 and :9 — same pattern... docs/ARCHITECTURE.md:5-7 itself names one sanctioned exception (app/billing/errors.py, which only imports the SDK's exception classes)

report_1.evidence: InvoiceOut (medibill/backend/app/models/invoice.py:16-22) has a sixth field, external_ref, added in commit c23da88... a field the doc doesn't mention at all.

report_2.evidence: The actual InvoiceOut dataclass in medibill/backend/app/models/invoice.py:16-22 has six fields: id, tenant_id, amount_cents, currency, status, external_ref. The external_ref field was added in commit c23da88

report_1.evidence: All of the above should land **in the same commit/PR that adds refund support**, not as a follow-up — this is exactly the pattern project documentation calls out: doc updates deferred to "later" is how a contract doc goes stale in the first place.

report_2.evidence: it's already three-plus weeks stale as of today (2026-07-11) — so it should be fixed now, immediately, as a small standalone doc correction, independent of the refund work... I'd do this **before or alongside** the refund implementation, not after

report_1.evidence: consider dropping the literal count ("4 webhook event types") from the prose entirely and just enumerating the types, so the doc can't silently disagree with the code on a number — the list of `if kind == ...` branches in `webhooks.py` is the actual source of truth and the doc's count will drift again the next time someone adds a type without reading this doc.

report_2.evidence: I would change the statement to say "5 webhook event types" and add payment_pending to the list.
